inspectionMethod: Visual inspection for cracking, weeping; tap test for adhesion.
notes: Polyurethane sealant degrades from UV and fuel permeation; reseal at condition 4 to prevent leaks and contamination.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection for cracking, weeping; tap test for adhesion.
notes: Polyurethane sealant degrades from UV and fuel permeation; reseal at condition 4 to prevent leaks and contamination.

inspectionMethod: Remove cap; inspect for cracking, hardening, loss of elasticity.
notes: Nitrile O-ring hardens from heat and ozone; replace when cracked or cannot form watertight seal.

inspectionMethod: Remove cap; inspect for cracking, hardening, loss of elasticity.
notes: Nitrile O-ring hardens from heat and ozone; replace when cracked or cannot form watertight seal.

inspectionMethod: Blow air through vent line under 5 psi; listen for flow and check for debris in outlet.
notes: Blockage from ice, nesting, or fuel gum restricts airflow and causes vaporlock or tank collapse on descent.

inspectionMethod: Blow air through vent line under 5 psi; listen for flow and check for debris in outlet.
notes: Blockage from ice, nesting, or fuel gum restricts airflow and causes vaporlock or tank collapse on descent.

inspectionMethod: Remove sender unit; inspect float for cracks, oil saturation, corrosion on pivot pin.
notes: Foam float absorbs fuel and water, becoming buoyant-challenged; mechanical stuck pivot or cracked float causes gauge errors.

inspectionMethod: Remove sender unit; inspect float for cracks, oil saturation, corrosion on pivot pin.
notes: Foam float absorbs fuel and water, becoming buoyant-challenged; mechanical stuck pivot or cracked float causes gauge errors.

inspectionMethod: Multimeter across sender terminals at empty (240 Ω) and full (33 Ω); drift >2 Ω indicates replacement needed.
notes: Rheostat wiper contact corrosion and water contamination cause resistance drift beyond ±2 Ω, falsifying fuel quantity indication.

inspectionMethod: Multimeter across sender terminals at empty (240 Ω) and full (33 Ω); drift >2 Ω indicates replacement needed.
notes: Rheostat wiper contact corrosion and water contamination cause resistance drift beyond ±2 Ω, falsifying fuel quantity indication.

inspectionMethod: Fuel sample from tank sump; water-finding paste or Karl Fischer titration.
notes: Condensation and cap seal leakage introduce water; >50 ppm triggers maintenance, >200 ppm requires tank drain and inspection.

inspectionMethod: Fuel sample from tank sump; water-finding paste or Karl Fischer titration.
notes: Condensation and cap seal leakage introduce water; >50 ppm triggers maintenance, >200 ppm requires tank drain and inspection.

inspectionMethod: Fuel sample culture or rapid ATP test; visual inspection for tank water layer and sediment.
notes: Bacteria and fungi thrive at water–fuel interface; >100 CFU/mL indicates biocide treatment; >1000 requires tank cleaning.

inspectionMethod: Fuel sample culture or rapid ATP test; visual inspection for tank water layer and sediment.
notes: Bacteria and fungi thrive at water–fuel interface; >100 CFU/mL indicates biocide treatment; >1000 requires tank cleaning.

inspectionMethod: Torque wrench check on all attach screws (5 per tank); inspect screw holes for stripping.
notes: Vibration and thermal cycling loosen screws; torque to 35 ft-lb per Cessna SRM; <32 ft-lb indicates screw slip and leak risk.

inspectionMethod: Torque wrench check on all attach screws (5 per tank); inspect screw holes for stripping.
notes: Vibration and thermal cycling loosen screws; torque to 35 ft-lb per Cessna SRM; <32 ft-lb indicates screw slip and leak risk.

inspectionMethod: Pull each of five sump drains per wing; inspect valve seats for debris and corrosion; drain fluid clarity.
notes: Ten sump valves total (5 L, 5 R) drain water and sediment daily; seized or leaking valve prevents proper sumping.

inspectionMethod: Blow-test check valve from tank side (10 psi); should hold pressure; reverse (vent side) should open freely.
notes: Stuck closed causes vaporlock or tank collapse; stuck open allows fuel siphoning and vapors into vent system.

inspectionMethod: Remove gascolator bowl; inspect 100-mesh screen for rust, lint, varnish; clean or replace if damaged.
notes: Screen plugging from varnish and particulates restricts flow and starves engine of fuel during climb or high power.

inspectionMethod: Remove bowl; inspect O-ring for cracks, hardening, loss of grip on bowl groove.
notes: Nitrile O-ring hardens and shrinks from fuel and temperature; replace every 5 years or if visibly cracked.

inspectionMethod: Drain bowl sediment into clear container; observe water separation; sample with water-finding paste.
notes: Bowl is lowest point in fuel system; water settles here and must be drained preflight; >30 ppm indicates tank moisture.

inspectionMethod: Cycle selector through all positions; feel for distinct detent clicks; check for slop at each position.
notes: Detent springs and ball weaken from vibration and wear; loose selector can slip mid-flight, causing fuel starvation.

inspectionMethod: Remove selector valve; inspect seat surfaces under magnification for pitting, corrosion, or particulate embedding.
notes: Particulate and corrosion pit valve seat; leakage through seat causes unintended fuel crossfeed between tanks.

inspectionMethod: Disconnect fuel line at engine and observe flow during engine-off gravity drain; compare to specification.
notes: Oxidation of fuel at high engine-bay temps deposits varnish on ID; >50% blockage starves engine and requires line replacement.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection under engine baffles; palpate line for cracks, heat damage, and rubbing marks on nearby structures.
notes: Vibration and heat abrade fuel line wall; pinhole leak in pressurized line at 8–16 psi causes fuel spray and fire risk.

inspectionMethod: Fuel sample and lab particle count; line interior borescope if accessible.
notes: Cooler wing environment slows varnish formation, but old fuel in static tanks allows oxidation; <5 condition warrants flushing.

inspectionMethod: Pump primer handle several times and observe for fuel seeping from pump body or control rod.
notes: Leather or synthetic seal shrinks from fuel; leakage allows fuel to vent into cabin or engine bay during priming.

inspectionMethod: Pull plunger fully; feel for stickiness and resistance; disassemble and inspect O-ring under magnification.
notes: O-ring loss of elasticity reduces pump seal and allows air ingress; weak or absent prime delays cold-start fuel delivery.

inspectionMethod: Compare gauge reading to airframe-mounted test gauge (0–16 psi Bourdon); tolerance ±0.5 psi at key RPMs.
notes: Bourdon tube calibration drifts from vibration and thermal cycling; >0.5 psi error from spec requires gauge overhaul or replacement.

inspectionMethod: Remove alternator; disassemble and measure brush length with calipers; inspect for glazing and chip-out.
notes: Brush wear is normal but accelerated by high-current loads and poor slip ring condition; scrap at 0.250 in per TSO-C87.

inspectionMethod: Remove alternator; disassemble and measure brush length with calipers; inspect for glazing and chip-out.
notes: Brush wear is normal but accelerated by high-current loads and poor slip ring condition; scrap at 0.250 in per TSO-C87.

inspectionMethod: Remove brushes; hang scale from brush holder; measure load needed to pull brush against spring; standard 150 g.
notes: Spring relaxation reduces brush contact pressure on slip ring; <120 g causes chatter, sparking, and carbon deposition.

displayName: Alternator Slip Ring Wear and Eccentricity
inspectionMethod: Remove rotor; inspect slip rings under magnification for corrosion, pitting, flatspotting, groove wear; measure runout with dial indicator.
notes: Corrosion and brush chatter groove slip rings; high runout causes brush bounce and intermittent contact, reducing output.

inspectionMethod: Spin rotor by hand after disassembly; listen and feel for grinding or drag; inspect bearing cage for cracks.
notes: Ball bearing fatigue and lubrication breakdown cause noise and drag; seized bearing locks rotor and kills alternator output.

inspectionMethod: Spin rotor by hand after disassembly; listen and feel for grinding or drag; inspect bearing cage for cracks.
notes: Ball bearing fatigue and lubrication breakdown cause noise and drag; seized bearing locks rotor and kills alternator output.

inspectionMethod: Megohm-meter test between any winding and case at 500 VDC; read after 1-minute stabilization; standard >5 MΩ.
notes: Varnish breakdown from heat and humidity degrades winding-to-case insulation; <1 MΩ indicates short-to-ground and imminent failure.

displayName: Alternator Diode Trio Integrity (6 Diodes Combined)
inspectionMethod: Resistance test on alternator output; diodes open or shorted cause high output impedance or negative resistance.
notes: Thermal and electrical stress on rectifier diodes; any diode open-circuit = 33% output loss; closed circuit = reverse discharge.

inspectionMethod: Voltmeter across main bus at idle, 1000 RPM, and 2000 RPM; spec 27.5–28.5 V; >0.5 V drift = regulator replacement.
notes: Thermal drift in Zener reference and resistor tolerances; <27.5 V causes chronic battery undercharge and sulfation.

inspectionMethod: Raise engine RPM slowly while logging bus voltage; measure voltage difference between cut-in and cut-out points.
notes: Comparator hysteresis widens with component drift; >0.8 V causes cycling (on/off oscillation) and load transient stress.

inspectionMethod: Belt tension gauge midpoint between alternator and crank pulleys; spec 60–70 lbs new, 50–60 lbs used; replace if <50 lbs.
notes: Belt stretch and pulley wear reduce tension; <50 lbs causes slip, low output, and alternator undercharge of battery.

inspectionMethod: Visual and tactile inspection of belt OD and ID under running engine; search for radial and circumferential cracks.
notes: UV and ozone degrade belt material; circumferential cracks indicate imminent failure and belt replacement within 25 flight hours.

inspectionMethod: Monitor field CB for nuisance trips over 25 flight hours; inspect contact faces and reset mechanism for friction.
notes: Mechanical spring fatigue and contact erosion reduce breaker reliability; >2 nuisance trips in 25 hours warrants CB replacement.

inspectionMethod: Disconnect cable lug; measure resistance across lug and post with microhm-meter; inspect for verdigris or black corrosion.
notes: Copper–steel junction corrodes in humid salt air; >5 mΩ causes voltage drop and low charging current to battery.

inspectionMethod: Hydrometer (not AGM-suitable) or voltmeter under load; open-circuit voltage 26.5 V = full, <25 V = depleted.
notes: SoC state machine driven by alternator charge current and engine-off parasitic drain; <50% at night indicates charging deficiency.

inspectionMethod: Load test to 50A for 15 seconds at 26.5 V baseline; capacity deduction ~2.8 Ah per 0.1 V drop; <28 Ah = replace.
notes: Capacity fade from cycle count and sulfation is primary battery wear indicator; 80% (28 Ah) is service limit per Concorde specs.

inspectionMethod: AC milliohm-meter or vendor load-test; impedance test at 100 Hz typical; threshold 15 mΩ at 77F.
notes: Internal resistance rise from active material loss and paste degradation; >15 mΩ at 50A load causes excessive voltage sag.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection; corrosion score 0–10; remove cable and inspect post surface; wire brush test for ease of cleaning.
notes: Positive post oxidation is slow; green/blue patina acceptable; white/black crust indicates active corrosion requiring post cleaning.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection; corrosion score 0–10; remove cable and inspect post surface; wire brush test for ease of cleaning.
notes: Negative post corrosion often faster than positive due to sulfation; white/gray crust typical; <6 condition requires post refurbishment.

inspectionMethod: Torque wrench check on both posts; spec 18–22 ft-lb; loose lugs cause resistance heating and corrosion.
notes: Vibration and thermal cycling loosen terminal nuts; <16 ft-lb indicates slip and contact resistance growth.

inspectionMethod: Palpate strap for slack; attempt to lift battery by 1 inch under strap; movement >0.5 inch indicates retightening needed.
notes: Strap relaxation allows battery vibration; excessive motion can fracture terminals and cause intermittent electrical faults.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of vent tube routing and outlet; blow gently through tube to confirm patency; check for kinks/crushing.
notes: Blocked vent tube prevents recombinant gas escape during overcharge; AGM seals allow <0.1 psi buildup before safety vent opens.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of all six case sides; feel for soft spots or warping; measure height and width with calipers.
notes: Cracks allow electrolyte escape; swelling indicates internal pressure buildup from failed vent or overcharge; replace immediately.

inspectionMethod: Check manufacturing date code on battery case; calculate months/years in service from first installation date.
notes: Concorde AGM life expectancy is 5–7 years in average service; beyond 8 years expect capacity loss and replacement advisability.

inspectionMethod: Infer from duty cycle history (overnight parked without alternator, cold starts <-10C); log events in maintenance records.
notes: Deep discharge (SoC <20%) each count as 1 cycle; >200 cycles in 5 years indicates hard use; 400+ cycles = scheduled replacement.

inspectionMethod: Lab acid-soluble sulfate test or vendor proprietary sulfation imaging; index 0–100 scale; >60 indicates >30% capacity loss.
notes: Sulfation (lead sulfate crystal formation) from undercharge and deep discharge is partly reversible; >60 index = capacity loss likely permanent.

inspectionMethod: Safety vent integrity inspection; measure case pressure after 10A charge for 1 hour; should not exceed 5 psi.
notes: AGM glass separator traps recombinant H2/O2 and forces reabsorption; seal failure allows gas to vent instead of recombine.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of battery tray and box aluminum; look for white oxide film and pitting; measure thickness if severe.
notes: Aluminum tray corrodes in salt air; corrosion layer increases ground bond resistance and can impede heat dissipation from battery.

inspectionMethod: Measure voltage drop across bus bar at 50A load current; calculate resistance by Ohm's Law; inspect for oxidation.
notes: Oxidation and corrosion of copper bus laminations increase resistance; >3 mΩ causes excessive voltage drop and heat generation.

displayName: Main Bus Bar Oxidation and Discoloration
inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of bus bar under cowling; look for black oxidation layer; scratch surface to assess depth of corrosion.
notes: Copper oxide (black) layer acts as insulator and resistor; >50% surface coverage warrants polishing or bar replacement.

inspectionMethod: Megohm-meter test from coil terminals to contactor frame at 500 VDC; spec >2 MΩ; varnish cracks indicate imminent failure.
notes: Insulation breakdown from moisture and thermal cycling reduces coil pull force; <2 MΩ prevents reliable closure during cold starts.

inspectionMethod: Disassemble contactor; measure contact separation and surface condition; standard 0.050 in; service limit 0.045 in.
notes: Contact erosion from arcing during high-current startup transients; <0.040 in indicates contactor replacement to ensure reliable closure.

inspectionMethod: Megohm-meter test from coil terminals to contactor frame at 500 VDC; spec >2 MΩ; varnish cracks indicate imminent failure.
notes: Insulation breakdown from moisture and thermal cycling reduces coil pull force; <2 MΩ prevents reliable closure during cold starts.

inspectionMethod: Disassemble contactor; measure contact separation and surface condition; standard 0.050 in; service limit 0.045 in.
notes: Contact erosion from arcing during high-current starter engagement; <0.040 in indicates contactor replacement to ensure reliable operation.

inspectionMethod: Four-wire resistance measurement from engine block to fuselage via primary and secondary strap; spec <10 mΩ new, <25 mΩ service.
notes: Strap corrosion and oxidation at interfaces increase impedance; >25 mΩ allows RF coupling and autopilot interference paths.

inspectionMethod: Measure resistance between avionics ground reference and fuselage skin at multiple points; spec <5 mΩ typical.
notes: Fuselage corrosion and paint buildup degrade skin continuity; >15 mΩ allows asymmetric static discharge and instrument error.

inspectionMethod: Measure shunt resistance at known current (50A load); compare to spec (typically 0.0001 Ω); >5% drift indicates replacement.
notes: Temperature coefficient and mechanical stress on shunt alloy cause resistance creep; >5% error falsifies ammeter indication.

inspectionMethod: Precision load test at 0A, 25A, 50A, 100A; compare instrument reading to calibrated reference; tolerance ±5A across range.
notes: Mechanical friction and soft-iron magnet hysteresis cause needle lag and calibration drift; >10% error at any point indicates overhaul or replacement.

inspectionMethod: Thermal test chamber and calibrated load; ramp current until trip; spec tolerance ±10% of rated; beyond 15% indicates replacement.
notes: Bimetal strip relaxation and calibration spring drift shift CB trip threshold; >15% error increases short-circuit risk or false nuisance trips.

inspectionMethod: Push-button reset test; measure reset force and feel for sticky detent; inspect spring for stress marks under magnification.
notes: Mechanical spring fatigue from tens of thousands of load cycles; >6 uses (trip and reset) per flight in 25 hours warrants CB replacement.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of fuse element through case; look for blackening, silvering (arcing), or visible fractures; continuity test.
notes: ANL element silvering from arcing near limit; black coloration indicates overtemp; >3 fuses blown in 100 hours suggests electrical fault upstream.

inspectionMethod: Visual inspection of harness routing and sheathing; flex-test for brittleness; megohm test to ground for insulation breakdown.
notes: Thermal cycling and fuel/oil vapor attack polymeric insulation; >50 years age or <2 MΩ to ground indicates harness replacement.
